name: RandomTree.Uniform

unions.name: Tree
unions.comment: Type that represents binary tree with weighted elements for random picking up.

values.name: count
values.comment: Returns how many elements the tree has.

values.name: delete
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which is the same as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which fails to passes the test function given as the first parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has all elements in given list.

values.name: get
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree` given as parameter.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a data to an already-existing `Tree`.

values.name: insertList
values.comment: Inserts all elements of given list into the tree of second parameter. The first components of the tuple denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: List.List a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps all the content of the tree.

values.name: replace
values.comment: Random generator that replaces one weighted data from the tree and generates a pair consisting of the removed data and resultant tree.
values.type: a -> RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( a, RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has only one element.

values.name: take
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree`, paired with the rest part of the tree.
If the `Tree` has only one element, then `Nothing` is returned as the second component.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.
values.type: RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( a, Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Uniform.Tree a) )

name: RandomTree.Weighted

unions.name: Tree
unions.comment: Type that represents binary tree with weighted elements for random picking up.

aliases.name: Weighted
aliases.comment: Type alias that represents each weighted contents in `Tree`.

aliases.name: WideFloat
aliases.comment:
Type that represent floating point number with wider range than `Float` in core package. This is introduced for preventing overflow of exponentially changing value.
If you need to directly manipulate values of `WideFloat`, please hit `elm install kudzu-forest/elm-wide-float` in your terminal.

values.name: adjustContent
values.comment: Maps content in `Element`.

values.name: adjustWeight
values.comment: Returns weighted datum whose weight is multiplied with the given `Float`.

values.name: count
values.comment: Returns how many elements the tree has.

values.name: delete
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which is the same as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Removes any number of elements which passes the test function given as the first parameter from the second parameter. The returned value is wrapped in `Maybe`.
values.type: (RandomTree.Weighted.WideFloat -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has all element in given list.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a -> List.List (RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: fromPairs
values.comment:
Creates `Tree` from list of tuples of a `Float` value corresponding to its weight as the first component, and a datum as the second component.
the weighter it is, the higher probability it is chosen in.
values.type: ( Basics.Float, a ) -> List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: get
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree` given as parameter.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.

values.name: insert
values.comment: Inserts a Weighted data to an already-existing `Tree`.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: insertListWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts all elements of given list into the tree of second parameter. The first components of the tuple denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: List.List ( Basics.Float, a ) -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: insertWithRelativeWeight
values.comment: Inserts an element into the tree. The first parameter denotes the relative weight(`1` means the whole weight of the original tree).
values.type: Basics.Float -> a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps all the content of the tree, with the weights unchanged.

values.name: replace
values.comment: Random generator that replaces one weighted data from the tree and generates a pair consisting of the removed data and resultant tree.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a -> RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a, RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a )

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Returns a `Tree` that has only one element.

values.name: take
values.comment:
Random generator that generates one of the elements conteined in `Tree`, paired with the rest part of the tree.
If the `Tree` has only one element, then `Nothing` is returned as the second component.
The time complexity is _O(log(N))_ where _N_ denotes the size of the tree.
values.type: RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a -> Random.Generator ( RandomTree.Weighted.Weighted a, Maybe.Maybe (RandomTree.Weighted.Tree a) )

values.name: totalWeight
values.comment: Returns the sum of weight of all the elements in the tree.
